channel.title: The First Officer's Log
channel.description: Briefings from your first officer: 'what happened since' update logs and dispatches, compiled for the Captain.
channel.itunes:author: Captain
channel.itunes:summary: Briefings from your first officer: 'what happened since' update logs and dispatches, compiled for the Captain.

item.title: News from the Beacon - July 12, 2026
item.description: Your ~3-minute signal from the long tail - the specific threads the front page skips. This issue: the Supreme Court blocks the firing of a Fed governor (and the shadow-docket fight underneath it), Anthropic seats former Fed chair Ben Bernanke as Claude Sonnet 5 ships, a deepening PLA purge that analysts say may be pushing back Beijing's Taiwan timetable, TSMC's 2nm capacity sold out, Louisiana's whooping cranes raising pasta-named chicks in the crawfish fields, Subnautica 2's Adaptive Measures update, and a goblin shark filmed alive in the wild for the first time. First officer, out.
item.itunes:summary: Your ~3-minute signal from the long tail - the specific threads the front page skips. This issue: the Supreme Court blocks the firing of a Fed governor (and the shadow-docket fight underneath it), Anthropic seats former Fed chair Ben Bernanke as Claude Sonnet 5 ships, a deepening PLA purge that analysts say may be pushing back Beijing's Taiwan timetable, TSMC's 2nm capacity sold out, Louisiana's whooping cranes raising pasta-named chicks in the crawfish fields, Subnautica 2's Adaptive Measures update, and a goblin shark filmed alive in the wild for the first time. First officer, out.

item.title: The Update Log #2 - Radiolab Follow-Ups (Jul 2026)
item.description: Update Log #2 - seven Radiolab threads you flagged, chased to 2026 and fact-checked. The Supreme Court's legitimacy crisis (the ethics scandals, the toothless new ethics code, the stalled reforms); Candida auris going from mystery to entrenched hospital pathogen; how the LLM boom reshapes the 'no perfect translation' argument; how COVID became the deadliest US pandemic by raw count and what 1918 warns about forgetting; what became of biologist Brandon Ogbunu; how the 'major questions doctrine' - not the Commerce Clause - quietly rewired federal power, then struck down Trump's tariffs; and the embattled afterlife of radio artist Joe Frank. First officer, out.
item.itunes:summary: Update Log #2 - seven Radiolab threads you flagged, chased to 2026 and fact-checked. The Supreme Court's legitimacy crisis (the ethics scandals, the toothless new ethics code, the stalled reforms); Candida auris going from mystery to entrenched hospital pathogen; how the LLM boom reshapes the 'no perfect translation' argument; how COVID became the deadliest US pandemic by raw count and what 1918 warns about forgetting; what became of biologist Brandon Ogbunu; how the 'major questions doctrine' - not the Commerce Clause - quietly rewired federal power, then struck down Trump's tariffs; and the embattled afterlife of radio artist Joe Frank. First officer, out.

item.title: Wormhole 1 - The Magic Number
item.description: The first Wormhole - a produced deep-dive. Seven lines of code in a 1999 video game (Quake III) compute one-over-a-square-root about four times faster than should be possible - no square root, no division - and the programmer left a comment beside it that is, simply, a bewildered curse. When the source went public, nobody at id Software would claim it. Follow the thread all the way down: why a number's raw bits are secretly its own logarithm, a magic constant nobody can explain the origin of, a decade-long hunt for the author, and a payoff that loops back to the standard underneath every calculation your computer makes. (The fast inverse square root, 0x5f3759df.)
item.itunes:summary: The first Wormhole - a produced deep-dive. Seven lines of code in a 1999 video game (Quake III) compute one-over-a-square-root about four times faster than should be possible - no square root, no division - and the programmer left a comment beside it that is, simply, a bewildered curse. When the source went public, nobody at id Software would claim it. Follow the thread all the way down: why a number's raw bits are secretly its own logarithm, a magic constant nobody can explain the origin of, a decade-long hunt for the author, and a payoff that loops back to the standard underneath every calculation your computer makes. (The fast inverse square root, 0x5f3759df.)

item.title: Knowledge Voyage 2 - How GPS Knows Where You Are
item.description: The V3 explainer redeems the 'teach the real terms' note: a zero-to-understanding voyage through how your phone finds you - trilateration (not triangulation), atomic clocks, turning time into distance, and the showstopper - the special- and general-relativity time dilation (+45 and -7 = +38 microseconds/day) that GPS must correct or drift ~10 km/day. Plus ephemeris vs almanac and the GNSS family (GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou). Anchored in Captain's eBird coordinates: every checklist pin placed by four atomic clocks and a dead physicist riding shotgun.
item.itunes:summary: The V3 explainer redeems the 'teach the real terms' note: a zero-to-understanding voyage through how your phone finds you - trilateration (not triangulation), atomic clocks, turning time into distance, and the showstopper - the special- and general-relativity time dilation (+45 and -7 = +38 microseconds/day) that GPS must correct or drift ~10 km/day. Plus ephemeris vs almanac and the GNSS family (GPS, GLONASS, Galileo, BeiDou). Anchored in Captain's eBird coordinates: every checklist pin placed by four atomic clocks and a dead physicist riding shotgun.

item.title: News from the Beacon - July 10 Evening Signal
item.description: Six signals across Captain's worlds, all verified: Subnautica 2's first big Early Access update (and the Krafton lawsuit settling in the devs' favor); Claude Sonnet 5 shipping as the new default; Japan's Hayabusa2 flyby of asteroid Torifune (whose name means 'bird ship'); pied-flycatcher migration steered by both genes and upbringing; NOAA's ~7,000-sq-mi Gulf dead-zone forecast; and the myth-busting that a queen bee is crowned by her nursery, not by royal jelly.
item.itunes:summary: Six signals across Captain's worlds, all verified: Subnautica 2's first big Early Access update (and the Krafton lawsuit settling in the devs' favor); Claude Sonnet 5 shipping as the new default; Japan's Hayabusa2 flyby of asteroid Torifune (whose name means 'bird ship'); pied-flycatcher migration steered by both genes and upbringing; NOAA's ~7,000-sq-mi Gulf dead-zone forecast; and the myth-busting that a queen bee is crowned by her nursery, not by royal jelly.

item.title: Knowledge Voyage 1 - The Whole Family Tree of Birds
item.description: The first V3 explainer: your First Officer maps the entire bird family tree - from the birds-are-dinosaurs reframe to the post-asteroid boomtown of Neoaves - anchored entirely in birds from Captain's own life list (Louisiana waders, the songbird supernova). Learn the neighborhoods, not the 11,000 species, plus the twists where looks lie: the falcon is a parrot's cousin, the flamingo a grebe's. A scaffolded, zero-to-understanding voyage.
item.itunes:summary: The first V3 explainer: your First Officer maps the entire bird family tree - from the birds-are-dinosaurs reframe to the post-asteroid boomtown of Neoaves - anchored entirely in birds from Captain's own life list (Louisiana waders, the songbird supernova). Learn the neighborhoods, not the 11,000 species, plus the twists where looks lie: the falcon is a parrot's cousin, the flamingo a grebe's. A scaffolded, zero-to-understanding voyage.

item.title: News from the Beacon - July 10, 2026
item.description: Pilot news segment. Four signals from Captain's worlds, verified with dates and sources: whooping cranes raising chicks in Jefferson Davis Parish (flock up to ~75); a draft plan to rebuild Raccoon Island, a pelican/tern/skimmer colony off the Terrebonne-Lafourche coast; Anthropic's July 6 'global workspace' interpretability finding inside Claude; and NASA Lucy's wobbling peanut asteroid with signs of ancient water. Plus a wry note on podcast launches hitting an eight-year low.
item.itunes:summary: Pilot news segment. Four signals from Captain's worlds, verified with dates and sources: whooping cranes raising chicks in Jefferson Davis Parish (flock up to ~75); a draft plan to rebuild Raccoon Island, a pelican/tern/skimmer colony off the Terrebonne-Lafourche coast; Anthropic's July 6 'global workspace' interpretability finding inside Claude; and NASA Lucy's wobbling peanut asteroid with signs of ancient water. Plus a wry note on podcast launches hitting an eight-year low.

item.title: The Update Log #1 — Radiolab Follow-Ups (Jul 2026)
item.description: Your first mate chases nine threads from the Radiolab episodes you just heard, all the way up to 2026: the post-Heller gun rulings (Bruen, Rahimi, and the AR-15 case), Prigozhin's mutiny and fall (and the whistleblower we couldn't find), Uvalde and the 'no duty to protect' doctrine, the horseshoe-crab synthetic that arrived but didn't stick, Meta's moderation reversal, the Smarty Plants replication that failed, the Carlisle children coming home, Mississippi's new flag, and where Jad Abumrad went after Radiolab. Researched and fact-checked; where the original episodes got something wrong from a 2026 view, we say so.
item.itunes:summary: Your first mate chases nine threads from the Radiolab episodes you just heard, all the way up to 2026: the post-Heller gun rulings (Bruen, Rahimi, and the AR-15 case), Prigozhin's mutiny and fall (and the whistleblower we couldn't find), Uvalde and the 'no duty to protect' doctrine, the horseshoe-crab synthetic that arrived but didn't stick, Meta's moderation reversal, the Smarty Plants replication that failed, the Carlisle children coming home, Mississippi's new flag, and where Jad Abumrad went after Radiolab. Researched and fact-checked; where the original episodes got something wrong from a 2026 view, we say so.
